name: OpsLevel MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official OpsLevel MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with OpsLevel's service catalog and internal developer platform — querying service metadata, checking maturity scorecards, managing ownership, and accessing service health and compliance data.
use_cases: Querying service ownership and metadata from platform engineering agents
Checking service maturity scorecard status from compliance agents
Discovering service dependencies from architecture analysis agents
Updating service metadata and documentation from DevOps automation agents
Monitoring SLO/SLA compliance from reliability agents
Identifying services needing infrastructure upgrades from platform agents
not_for: Teams using Backstage, Cortex, or Port as their IDP
Simple service inventory without maturity tracking
best_when: An agent needs to query service catalog data, check maturity levels, or manage platform health in an OpsLevel-powered organization.
avoid_when: You're using Backstage, Cortex, or Port as your IDP.

auth.notes: OpsLevel API tokens at account level. No scope granularity. GraphQL API is the primary interface.

pricing.notes: Per-service pricing model. Enterprise for large organizations. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL is the primary API — agents need GraphQL query knowledge
Service aliases and IDs differ — use aliases for human-readable queries
Maturity checks take time to recalculate after changes
API token has no scope granularity — full org read/write
Scorecard levels are custom-defined per organization
Service catalog data may lag real-time infra state
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token lacks scopes. SOC 2, GDPR. Single-tenant deployment model.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token lacks scopes. SOC 2, GDPR.
